episode_event_salon_pasiosalon_date_ch02:
One day, Akari asked
[Name:PlayerNickname ] to come
meet her.

Thank you for coming,
[Name:PlayerNickname ].
So, the reason I wanted to talk to you is...

I wanted to thank you for always looking out
for me.

When I first came to Pasio, there was a lot I
didn’t know, and I was anxious.

But [Name:PlayerNickname ], you always
looked out for me, and your kindness really
gave me strength.

And that’s why I wanted to do something to
thank you!

So how about this: I’m good at crafting, so I
could teach you, and you could try making
something!

In this day and age, you can get all kinds of
nice things anytime you want.

But that’s exactly why handmade things have
their own charm!

That said, making stuff by hand does take
more time and effort.

And it’s hard to make something higher
quality than what you can buy in a store.
Maybe you don’t need anything like that...
(Tell her you still want to try.)
R-really?
If it could help you, it’d make me so happy!
(Tell her you like handmade things.)
Really? I’m so happy to hear that!

OK, as an expert crafter, I’ll teach you some
tips for making things by hand.

When we’re done, it’d be nice to eat some
potato mochi.

Hisuian potato mochi is made with
sootfoot roots.

Back in Jubilife Village, we often ate it after
missions.

I’ve been wanting to recreate that Hisui taste
here on Pasio.

I want you to try it, too, so I’ll do my best to
make some soon!
A few days later...

How is your crafting coming along,
[Name:PlayerNickname ]?
It takes some time to get used to...

but experience is the best teacher,
so keep trying!

By the way, I made some potato
mochi today!
Do you remember when we talked about it?

Right after, I tried to recreate the taste
of Hisui.

But unfortunately, I couldn’t get sootfoot
roots like we had in the Hisui region.

I tried subbing in different ingredients, and I
made something close, but to be honest, I’m
not so sure about it.

O-on second thought, I’ll hold off on giving
it to you!

If I can make it better next time, I’ll bring
you some!
(Tell her you’d rather try some now.)
W-well, if you’re sure...
(Tell her to be confident in her skills.)

Y-yeah... I made it, and I AM good at
making things by hand!

OK, got it. I went through all that trouble,
so here, have some!

Ah, but it’s a little embarrassing...
Would you mind eating it later?

Next time we see each other, tell me what
you think, OK?

In the meantime, I’ll work hard to make it
even tastier!
